title: Instability deepens in southern Lebanon after French peacekeeper killed in Hezbollah-blamed attack
subhead: Staff Sergeant Florian Montorio, a UNIFIL soldier, was killed on Saturday in an ambush while clearing mines from a road. French President Emmanuel Macron called on the Lebanese authorities 'to identify and prosecute those responsible without delay.'

The gold medal for deception: How a professor created an international award out of thin air
subhead: In 2016, Florent Montaclair stepped into the spotlight when he received the Gold Medal of Philology, a sort of Nobel Prize for the discipline. In fact, the award was created by Montaclair himself. It took an investigation by Romanian journalists to uncover this hoax, which is now the subject of a judicial inquiry in France.

title: German Chancellor Merz's coalition is fractured by intense internal conflict
subhead: After simmering for a long time, the hostility between Social Democrat Lars Klingbeil and Christian Democrat Katherina Reiche has recently burst forward over fuel price issues, forcing the chancellor to step in to de-escalate tensions.

title: Libya: 15 years after Gaddafi's fall, Marshal Haftar's authoritarian model spreads
subhead: From Benghazi, the de facto capital of eastern Libya, Khalifa Haftar, who calls himself the 'savior' of the nation, has extended his grip over three-quarters of the country, combining a firm security approach with infrastructure development.

title: Stuffed Cabbage World Championship brings together top culinary talent
subhead: From Seoul to Tokyo, London and Saskatoon in Canada, seven carefully selected contestants traveled to France at the end of February to take part in the second edition of the World Stuffed Cabbage Championship. Under the meticulous eyes of Michelin-starred chefs, Frenchman Olivier Caillon was named the winner.

subhead: Sing a duet, wander through the Musée d'Orsay, marvel at the Musée du Quai Branly, then hop on a scooter from one covered passageway to another… There is no shortage of fun activities in the Paris region to keep kids and teenagers entertained.

title: Ireland, striving to become Europe's data center hub, falls victim to its own success
subhead: Despite major pressure on the power grid and the risk of rising greenhouse gas emissions, Dublin wants to keep building these facilities for tech giants and establish itself as a European leader in the sector.

title: The turbulent life of Pavel Talankin, the Oscar-winning director in the Russian government's crosshairs
subhead: The global release of 'Mr. Nobody Against Putin,' which exposes Russian war propaganda to primary school students in the Urals, brought the film's director, Pavel Talankin, to public attention. But the Kremlin labeled him a 'foreign agent,' increasing the threats against the filmmaker, now living in exile in Prague.
